titles.ar: باب عِتْقِ الصَّغِيرِ وَ الشَّيْخِ الْكَبِيرِ وَ أَهْلِ الزَّمَانَاتِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Emancipation of the young and the old and aged, and the people with chronic illnesses

titles.ar: باب عِتْقِ الصَّغِيرِ وَ الشَّيْخِ الْكَبِيرِ وَ أَهْلِ الزَّمَانَاتِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Emancipation of the young and the old and aged, and the people with chronic illnesses

segments: كَتَبْتُ إِلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُعْتِقُ غُلَاماً صَغِيراً أَوْ شَيْخاً كَبِيراً أَوْ مَنْ بِهِ زَمَانَةٌ وَ مَنْ لَا حِيلَةَ لَهُ فَقَالَ مَنْ أَعْتَقَ مَمْلُوكاً لَا حِيلَةَ لَهُ فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَعُولَهُ حَتَّى يَسْتَغْنِيَ عَنْهُ وَ كَذَلِكَ كَانَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) يَفْعَلُ إِذَا أَعْتَقَ الصِّغَارَ وَ مَنْ لَا حِيلَةَ لَهُ .
arabic.text: كَتَبْتُ إِلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُعْتِقُ غُلَاماً صَغِيراً أَوْ شَيْخاً كَبِيراً أَوْ مَنْ بِهِ زَمَانَةٌ وَ مَنْ لَا حِيلَةَ لَهُ فَقَالَ مَنْ أَعْتَقَ مَمْلُوكاً لَا حِيلَةَ لَهُ فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَعُولَهُ حَتَّى يَسْتَغْنِيَ عَنْهُ وَ كَذَلِكَ كَانَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) يَفْعَلُ إِذَا أَعْتَقَ الصِّغَارَ وَ مَنْ لَا حِيلَةَ لَهُ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub who said,

‘I wrote to Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> and asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the man who emancipated a young boy, or an old Sheykh, one with chronic illness, and the one for whom there is no dodge for him. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The one who emancipates an owned slave for whom there is no means (of earning) for him, so upon him is that he shelters him until he is self-sufficient from him, and similar to that is what Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> did, when he<sup>asws</sup> emancipated the young and the one for whom there is no means (of earning)’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub who said,

‘I wrote to Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> and asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the man who emancipated a young boy, or an old Sheykh, one with chronic illness, and the one for whom there is no dodge for him. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The one who emancipates an owned slave for whom there is no means (of earning) for him, so upon him is that he shelters him until he is self-sufficient from him, and similar to that is what Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> did, when he<sup>asws</sup> emancipated the young and the one for whom there is no means (of earning)’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ قَالَ

titles.ar: باب عِتْقِ الصَّغِيرِ وَ الشَّيْخِ الْكَبِيرِ وَ أَهْلِ الزَّمَانَاتِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Emancipation of the young and the old and aged, and the people with chronic illnesses

segments: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الصَّبِيِّ يُعْتِقُهُ الرَّجُلُ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ قَدْ أَعْتَقَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) وِلْدَاناً كَثِيرَةً .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الصَّبِيِّ يُعْتِقُهُ الرَّجُلُ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ قَدْ أَعْتَقَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) وِلْدَاناً كَثِيرَةً .

segments: Muhammad, from Ahmad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, and Safwan Bin Yahya, from Al Aa’la Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>), said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the children whom the man emancipates. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, Ali<sup>asws</sup> had emancipated numerous children’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad, from Ahmad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, and Safwan Bin Yahya, from Al Aa’la Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>), said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the children whom the man emancipates. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, Ali<sup>asws</sup> had emancipated numerous children’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدٌ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ وَ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنِ الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ رَزِينٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا ( عليهما السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب عِتْقِ الصَّغِيرِ وَ الشَّيْخِ الْكَبِيرِ وَ أَهْلِ الزَّمَانَاتِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Emancipation of the young and the old and aged, and the people with chronic illnesses

segments: سَأَلْتُهُ عَمَّنْ أَعْتَقَ النَّسَمَةَ فَقَالَ أَعْتَقَ مَنْ أَغْنَى نَفْسَهُ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَمَّنْ أَعْتَقَ النَّسَمَةَ فَقَالَ أَعْتَقَ مَنْ أَغْنَى نَفْسَهُ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Mansour Bin Hazim, from Hisham Bin Salim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the emancipation of the women. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Emancipate one who can be self-sufficient herself’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Mansour Bin Hazim, from Hisham Bin Salim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the emancipation of the women. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Emancipate one who can be self-sufficient herself’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ
